reason: The mockup correctly presents a visual reference for the 'Circuit break... slice' option. It exhibits a clean, dark-mode themed 'Session Paused' page that communicates a circuit breaker state cleanly. The visual hierarchy clearly details the verifiable capability (Auth Middleware Refactor sub-task) along with its validation checks (unit tests passing, linting successful). The Gestalt principles of grouping and proximity are strongly respected (cards separate capability, total project progress, and circuit breaker history cleanly). The layout uses standard Tailwind v4 CSS classes, handles styling elegantly, and limits independent visual groups to under 9, strictly adhering to Miller's law. Colors are highly contrastive, meeting WCAG 2.1 AA thresholds for readability.
concerns: The atmospheric/background layout is slightly minimalist, which works well but can feel empty on wider desktop viewports (even though the content container is correctly limited to max-w-md).
Only a single mockup viewport is generated, meaning that we cannot fully audit a mobile 375px rendering side-by-side with a 1440px desktop view.

The primary action button ('Resume Full Session') and the secondary outline button ('Keep Progress & Exit') share a very similar text size (headline-sm), which could benefit from more distinctive visual contrast in sizing or styling.
